info.title: The PubMed Gazette API
info.description: Thin Rust proxy around NCBI E-utilities for the PubMed Gazette frontend.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Free-text fragment to look up in the MeSH thesaurus.

200.description: MeSH term suggestions for a free-text query

parameters.name: term
parameters.description:
Free-text PubMed query. Field tags (`[ti]`, `[au]`, `[mesh]`,
`[dp]`, …) and boolean operators (`AND`, `OR`, `NOT`) work as on
PubMed itself. Example: `crispr cas9` or `covid 2024[dp]`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
1-based page number. NCBI internally uses 0-based offsets; the
handler converts.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description:
Number of results per page. NCBI's hard ceiling is 10000 but
practical UIs stay ≤ 100.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sort order. One of: `relevance`, `pub_date`, `first_author`,
`journal`, `title`. Omitted = NCBI default (relevance).

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description:
Comma-separated raw PubMed filter expressions appended to `term`
as `(term) AND filter1 AND filter2 …`. Each fragment uses the
same `[tag]` syntax PubMed expects, e.g.
`humans[Filter],english[lang],2020:2025[dp],Review[pt]`.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description:
Comma-separated raw PubMed filter expressions (same shape as
`/api/search`).

parameters.name: max
parameters.description:
Maximum number of records to bundle into the export. NCBI's
per-call ceiling for `efetch` via History is 10,000; we clamp
to that. Default: 200.

200.description: Bulk export of the search result set. Response body type depends on the requested `format`: BibTeX text, CSV text, or a JSON array of ArticleDetail. The Content-Type header reflects this.

ArticleDetail.description:
Full article record parsed out of NCBI `efetch` XML.

Returned by `GET /api/article/{pmid}`. Compared to `Summary`,
this carries the abstract text, author affiliations, MeSH terms,
and author-supplied keywords.

abstract_text.description:
Full abstract. Multi-section abstracts (`BACKGROUND`, `METHODS`,
`RESULTS`, `CONCLUSIONS`, …) are joined with a blank line, each
section prefixed with its label. Empty string if no abstract is
indexed for this PMID.

authors.description: Authors with full names + affiliation strings, in published order.

doi.description: DOI without the `https://doi.org/` prefix. Empty if missing.

journal.description: Full journal title (e.g. `Cell communication and signaling : CCS`).

keywords.description:
Author-supplied keywords (NCBI calls these "Keyword List";
distinct from MeSH terms).

mesh_terms.description:
NLM-assigned MeSH descriptors (controlled vocabulary). Empty if
the record hasn't been indexed yet (recent articles often haven't).

pmid.description: PubMed unique identifier, echoed back from the request.

pubdate.description:
Publication date as `YYYY [Month [Day]]`, e.g. `2026 May 23`.
Components are omitted if NCBI didn't index them.

pubtypes.description:
Publication-type labels (`Journal Article`, `Review`, …). Same
shape as `Summary.pubtypes`.

title.description: Article title. May contain inline HTML tags from the source record.

Author.description:
One author record from `efetch` XML.

All fields default to empty string when NCBI omits them — typically
when an author has only a collective name, or when affiliation isn't
indexed.

affiliation.description:
Free-text affiliation string. May contain a full address, ORCID,
or email — we preserve NCBI's content verbatim.

fore_name.description: Given names + middle initials, space-separated (e.g. `John A`).

last_name.description: Family name as it appears in the article.

CiteResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/cite/{pmid}` — the same citation
rendered five different ways.

All formats are generated server-side from one `efetch` call and are
plain text (no HTML). Wrap in `<pre>` on the client if you want to
preserve line breaks (BibTeX is multi-line).

bibtex.description:
BibTeX entry, ready to paste into a `.bib` file. Multi-line.
The cite key is `pmid{PMID}`.

nlm.description:
NLM journal-citation style (similar to AMA; used by PubMed
itself).

ErrorResponse.description:
Wire-format error body returned for any non-2xx response.

The shape is intentionally tiny so it's easy to consume in JS:
`(await res.json()).message`. Pair it with the HTTP status code
for full context.

code.description:
Short machine-readable code suitable for switch-on logic.
Stable strings: `not_found`, `bad_request`, `internal`.

message.description:
Human-readable explanation. Safe to surface directly to the
end user — never includes stack traces or secrets.

count.description: Total matching MeSH terms in NCBI's mesh db (not capped to `limit`).

id.description: NCBI's MeSH unique identifier (UID). Stable across reindexes.

count.description:
Total matching records in PubMed for this query (the whole corpus,
not just the current page).

elapsed_ms.description:
Server-side wall-clock duration for the upstream NCBI roundtrip
(esearch + esummary), in milliseconds. Network latency from the
browser to this server is *not* included.

page.description: Echo of the requested page number (1-based).

page_size.description: Echo of the requested page size.

query_translation.description:
The query as NCBI rewrote it after MeSH expansion and synonym
substitution. Useful for showing the user "we actually searched
for X" beneath the search box.

Summary.description:
One row in the search results — the data NCBI `esummary` returns for
a single PMID, normalized.

Most string fields default to `""` when NCBI omits them rather than
being `Option<String>`, so the frontend can render unconditionally
without null checks.

authors.description:
Short author names in NCBI's display form
(`Last F`, e.g. `Liu Y`). Use `efetch_abstract` for full names.

doi.description:
DOI without the `https://doi.org/` prefix
(e.g. `10.1038/s41586-024-12345`). Empty if NCBI didn't index one.

epubdate.description:
Electronic-ahead-of-print date if applicable, otherwise empty.
Same free-text format as `pubdate`.

issue.description: Journal issue number within the volume.

lang.description: ISO 639-2 language code (`eng`, `jpn`, …). Empty if missing.

pubdate.description:
Publication date as NCBI formats it (e.g. `2026 May 23`,
`2024 Jun`). Free-text — *not* ISO-8601.

pubtypes.description:
Publication-type labels from NCBI
(e.g. `Journal Article`, `Review`, `Randomized Controlled Trial`).

title.description:
Article title. May contain inline HTML tags (`<i>`, `<sub>`, …)
that NCBI preserves from the original record.

tags.name: pubmed
tags.description: Search, fetch, and cite PubMed articles
